div.p: William by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, the quitclaim which Alan son of Walter my steward made to the same monks in my presence and full court, of the whole claim and every right which the said Alan or his heirs have had or ever could have had in the pasture on the western side of the Leader. Wherefore I will and instruct that the said monks of Melrose should hold the named land for ever and have it free and quit from every claim from the said Alan and his heirs, as freely and quitly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the said Alan son of Walter my steward bears witness. As witnesses, Jocelin bishop of Glasgow, John bishop of Dunkeld, Earl David my brother, Earl Duncan, Earl Gilbert, William de Morvillethe constable, William of Lindsay, Malcolm son of Earl Duncan, Walter of Berkeley, Robert of Berkeley, Henry of Graham. At Edinburgh.
